label: GCP Cloud Monitoring
description: Query metrics, logs, and alerts from Google Cloud Monitoring (formerly Stackdriver) using a service account.

form.title: Connect GCP Cloud Monitoring
form.subtitle: Authenticate with a GCP service account to read metrics and logs from your project.

methods.label: Service Account JSON
methods.description: Paste a GCP service account key file to grant Anton access to Cloud Monitoring metrics and logs. No browser pop-up required — just download the JSON key and paste it.
methods.how_to:
## Authenticate with a GCP Service Account

This is the simplest path: create a service account in Google Cloud Console, grant it the Monitoring Viewer role, download a JSON key, and paste it below.

### Steps

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and select the project you want to monitor.
2. Navigate to **IAM & Admin → Service Accounts → Create service account**.
3. Give it a name like `anton-monitoring` and click **Create and continue**.
4. Under **Grant this service account access to project**, assign the following roles:
   - `Monitoring Viewer` — read metrics and uptime checks
   - `Logs Viewer` — read log entries (optional, needed for log queries)
5. Click **Done**.
6. Click the service account you just created, go to the **Keys** tab, then **Add key → Create new key → JSON**.
7. The key file downloads automatically. Open it and copy the entire contents.
8. Paste the JSON into the **Service Account JSON** field below.
9. Enter your **GCP Project ID** (e.g. `my-project-123`) — found in the Cloud Console header or on the project dashboard.

### Notes

- The JSON key is shown only once at download time. If you lose it, delete the key and create a new one from the **Keys** tab.
- You can scope access further by using `Monitoring Metric Writer` or custom roles instead.
- To revoke access, delete the key from **IAM & Admin → Service Accounts → Keys**.

fields.name: service_account_json
fields.label: Service Account JSON
fields.description: Paste the full contents of the downloaded JSON key file for your GCP service account.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: GCP Project ID
fields.description: The Google Cloud project ID to query. Found in the Cloud Console header or on the project dashboard.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (User Account)
methods.description: Sign in with your Google account via OAuth to access Cloud Monitoring. Requires you to supply your own Google Cloud OAuth client ID and secret.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 — Sign in with Google

This method uses your personal or Workspace Google account. You'll need to create an OAuth client in Google Cloud Console first.

### Steps

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and select your project.
2. Go to **APIs & Services → Library** and enable the **Cloud Monitoring API** and **Cloud Logging API**.
3. Go to **APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen** — configure an internal or external app and add your email as a test user if external.
4. Go to **APIs & Services → Credentials → Create credentials → OAuth client ID** — choose **Desktop app** as the application type.
5. Copy the generated **Client ID** and **Client secret** into the fields below.
6. Enter the **GCP Project ID** you want to query.
7. Click **Submit** — your browser will open Google's consent screen.
8. Approve the requested scopes. Anton stores the resulting refresh token locally.

### Notes

- Refresh tokens can expire if not used for 6 months or if the OAuth consent screen is in testing mode. Re-authorize if you see auth errors.
- To revoke: visit [Third-party apps with account access](https://myaccount.google.com/permissions) and remove Anton's entry.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials. Use a Desktop app OAuth client.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID. Copied from the same row in Google Cloud Console Credentials.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: GCP Project ID
fields.description: The Google Cloud project ID to query. Found in the Cloud Console header or on the project dashboard.
